claims.statement: ADR-0009 delegates device secret generation and sealing to trusted production adapters, and ADR-0010 persists only the resulting sealedSecret without defining its cryptographic representation.

Node.js documents randomBytes as producing cryptographically strong pseudorandom data and timingSafeEqual as comparing underlying bytes with a constant-time algorithm while warning that surrounding code can still introduce timing leakage.

RFC 7636 recommends 32 random octets encoded as a 43-character base64url verifier and explains that a salt is unnecessary when the verifier already has 256 bits of entropy; FIPS 180-4 specifies SHA-256 but does not certify this runtime or composition.

H-007: Given successful Node.js cryptographic primitives, a 32-byte random device secret encoded as base64url and a versioned, domain-separated SHA-256 verifier compared only after strict parsing with a constant-time primitive can verify bearer possession without persisting bearer plaintext or a recovery key, while entropy, digest, token-format, and verifier-format failures abort issuance or fail authentication closed.

Review accepted H-007 only for the production secret generator and verifier adapter under successful Node.js cryptographic primitives after entropy-shape, domain-separation, plaintext-exclusion, token, digest, version, algorithm, canonical-format, constant-time-comparison-shape, primitive-failure, lifecycle-composition, rotation, and failed-issuance tests passed.

The accepted Slice cannot demonstrate cryptographic primitive correctness, FIPS validation, memory secrecy, host or process compromise resistance, transport protection, bearer disclosure recovery, endpoint authentication, request replay protection, rate limiting, in-flight revocation linearizability, multi-process storage, hardware integration, or the complete server composition root.

successCriteria.statement: Credential generation requests exactly 32 random bytes, emits a 43-character base64url secret and a protocol-valid identifier, and propagates entropy failure before a record is stored.
successCriteria.verification: Injected entropy length, token grammar, and entropy-failure lifecycle tests.

successCriteria.statement: Sealing accepts only the production device-token grammar and emits one explicit version and algorithm with a canonical 32-byte base64url digest over a device-specific domain and the complete bearer.
successCriteria.verification: Domain input, strict token grammar, verifier grammar, and plaintext-exclusion tests.

successCriteria.statement: Matching accepts only the exact supported version, algorithm, canonical digest length, and token grammar, then invokes the comparison primitive with two equal 32-byte values; all substitutions and primitive failures return false.
successCriteria.verification: Token, digest, version, algorithm, malformed-value, digest-failure, and comparison-shape refutation tests.

successCriteria.statement: The adapter composes with the existing lifecycle so a current token authenticates, rotation invalidates its predecessor, and neither issuance nor failed sealing leaves bearer plaintext in the store.
successCriteria.verification: Memory-store lifecycle composition, rotation, and failed-issuance tests.

successCriteria.statement: The Slice claims only production secret generation and verifier behavior under successful Node.js primitives; surrounding runtime, transport, endpoint, replay, rate-limit, hardware, and certification guarantees remain unknown.

definitionOfDone.statement: CHG-0016 records SDD-007 as an accepted security experiment and separates external facts, H-007, the reviewed inference, and unresolved guarantees.
The Slice adds only a server-side cryptographic adapter, focused tests, and the accepted decision; no route, socket, UI, store migration, or hardware adapter is added.
Review attempts to refute H-007 through entropy, digest, token, verifier, algorithm, version, substitution, comparison-shape, lifecycle, and rotation cases.
ADR-0011 was created only after the human authority accepted H-007 within the demonstrated production-codec boundary.
definitionOfDone.evidence: ADR-0011 context and Human Doctrine review

definitionOfDone.statement: Governance, focused server quality, full repository checks, and builds pass for the accepted Slice manifest.
definitionOfDone.evidence: npm run governance:check, server checks, npm run check, and npm run build
